recoveryBootstrapTab.targetConfigOverridesAreOptionalAndOnlyA: Target config overrides are optional and only apply when the token stores them.

profiles.title: Backup Profiles
profiles.subtitle: Reusable "what to protect" selections for device classes. Assign a profile through a configuration policy's Backup tab.
profiles.emptyBody: A profile bundles data sources (files, System State, SQL Server, Hyper-V) with their options so one selection protects a whole device class. Start from a template:
profiles.templatePickPrompt: Pick a starting point. Everything stays editable — the template just pre-fills the editor.
profiles.tmplServerDesc: Files (C:\Users), System State, and full SQL Server backups. The all-rounder for production servers.
profiles.tmplWindowsWsDesc: User data under C:\Users with temp files, browser caches, and the Recycle Bin excluded.
profiles.tmplMacosWsDesc: User data under /Users with caches, Trash, and local iPhone backups excluded.
profiles.tmplLinuxServerDesc: /home and /etc with caches and Trash excluded.
profiles.tmplBlankDesc: Start from scratch and enable exactly the sources you need.
profiles.editorSubtitle: A profile defines what gets protected. Schedules, retention, and destinations are set where the profile is assigned.
profiles.scopeOrgHint: Only visible and assignable within the current organization.
profiles.scopePartnerHint: Partner-wide: assignable in every organization, including ones created later. Requires full partner access.
profiles.activeDescription: Inactive profiles stay on existing policies but can't be newly assigned.
profiles.sourcesSubtitle: Enable each source this profile protects. A policy using this profile runs one backup job per enabled source.
profiles.needSource: Enable at least one data source
profiles.needPaths: File backups need at least one path
profiles.fileDesc: Back up selected directories and files.
profiles.systemStateDesc: Capture registry, boot files, and system components (Windows).
profiles.includeSystemStateDesc: Registry, boot files, and system components.
profiles.sqlDesc: Back up discovered SQL Server databases on the device.
profiles.hypervDesc: Back up discovered Hyper-V virtual machines on the host.
profiles.deleteInUse_one: This profile is used by {{count}} configuration policy. Unlink it there first.
profiles.deleteInUse_other: This profile is used by {{count}} configuration policies. Unlink it there first.
